and I work at a cemetery.

I had nightmares for years, and still to this day when I talk about it in full,

It basically just floods over me.

the subconscious of my mind, when I go to sleep, becomes very active.
and you know
all I can

it's,
it's diminished.

like the,

but I do remember that there were leaves coming down
but not as much as
a full-fledged fall. It was
more of like at the end of summer where they just start
changing and you got a few leaves coming off here and there
but they were really beautiful colors up there. In Southwest Virginia
we have some very, very beautiful colors
coming out in autumn and fall.
But it was me, my little brother, and we had a best friend at that time that had moved from,
I think what he told us was Pensacola, Florida.
I can't really remember where he told us he was from,
but I do remember that he said that he was from a city.
He had never been out into the country.
He had never been up to this part of the United States, the East Coast, the mountains, you know.
And he was really interested in going camping.
And like I said, I was 16.
My brother, my little brother, his name was Rob.
He was 13.
And John, he was a year older than I was.
So he was 17.
So it's me, my little brother Rob, and John.
I remember us talking in school and us planning on a camping trip, and we went ahead and planned it for the weekend.
Well, before I get into that, I'll kind of paint you a picture of the lay of the land.
The lay of the land was like this.
My mother and father had bought a double-wide, and on the side of a hill, down in a holler,
my dad, his specialty was actually cutting building pads out of the side of mountains
and doing road dirt work and stuff like that.
And later on in my life, that's what I learned how to do.
But if you can imagine looking down a valley, and one on the right side of the valley,
you cut a backwards L out of the side of that mountain,
And that's where we placed our double-wide.
Now, behind our double-wide was kind of steep.
And at that point, our double-wide had only been there about a year or a year and a half, maybe not even that long.
But I do remember that the dirt, there was still no foliage or plants or anything growing up directly behind the double-wide going up the mountain.
Well, up the mountain behind our double-wide was the camping spot.
And me and my brother had gone up there and scouted out a couple times.
And we had actually went up there a few times and camped.
And we'd never had anything happen up there, nothing suspicious, anything like that.
So if you went down the road, the main road, it's called Hollow Road.
It's off of Route 460.
You'll go down a hill into this holler, and it basically just goes straight and then down an incline, and then it levels out.
You go about a quarter to a half a mile, maybe three-quarters of a mile down that road.
And then if you look to the right, the road that you're driving on is on the left side of the valley.
and the valley, you know, as you're driving, you would take a right to go down into our driveway
and our driveway went down and it meandered to the right just a bit

and some longer grass, taller grass that kind of covered it.

little road, it's

that kind of meanders down the mountain down to our driveway.
